title: Srikanta
author: Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay
description: A classic Bengali novel following the wandering life of Srikanta through love, loss and self-discovery.

title: Pather Panchali
author: Bibhutibhushan Bandyopadhyay
description: A poignant tale of rural Bengal depicting childhood, poverty and the passage of time.

title: Char Adhyay
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: A novel exploring the tension between political idealism and personal love in colonial India.

title: Lalsalu
author: Syed Waliullah
description: A gripping story of religious exploitation in rural Bangladesh centered around a false shrine.

title: Aranyak
author: Bibhutibhushan Bandyopadhyay
description: A lyrical account of man's relationship with untamed nature in the forests of Jharkhand.

title: Dighi Kotha
author: Humayun Ahmed
description: Humayun Ahmed's vivid portrayal of Bangladeshi village life and its timeless human drama.

title: Kabi
author: Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay
description: The story of Nitai, a poet of lower caste, and his dignified yet tragic life journey.

title: Datta
author: Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay
description: A moving narrative of social injustice, sacrifice and the resilience of the human spirit.

title: Grihadaha
author: Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay
description: An intense exploration of love, duty and societal expectations in early 20th-century Bengal.

title: Naukadubi
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: A tale of mistaken identity and star-crossed love set against the backdrop of the Ganges.

title: Sanchayita
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: A celebrated anthology of Tagore's most beloved Bengali poems spanning his entire poetic career.

title: Banalata Sen
author: Jibanananda Das
description: Iconic modernist poems including the legendary Banalata Sen, exploring beauty, solitude and longing.

title: Sonar Tari
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: A seminal collection of poems reflecting on creation, mortality, and the eternal flow of time.

title: Dinguli Mor
author: Kazi Nazrul Islam
description: Fiery and tender verses by the rebel poet Nazrul, full of passion, patriotism and spirituality.

title: Chitrangada
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: A lyrical dance-drama in verse form exploring themes of identity, beauty and unconditional love.

title: Rupshi Bangla
author: Jibanananda Das
description: Poems celebrating the serene landscape of rural Bengal with unparalleled natural imagery.

title: Agnibeena
author: Kazi Nazrul Islam
description: Nazrul's debut poetry collection igniting revolution, romanticism and spiritual fire.

title: Kobita Samagra
author: Shamsur Rahman
description: Comprehensive collection of poems by Bangladesh's most celebrated post-independence poet.

title: Manasi
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: An introspective poetry collection where Tagore reflects on imagination, idealism and the self.

title: Pratidiner Shabda
author: Al Mahmud
description: Al Mahmud's lyrical poems drawing on river imagery, folklore and rural Bangladeshi life.

title: Misir Ali Omnibus
author: Humayun Ahmed
description: A collection of psychological mysteries featuring the brilliant and eccentric professor Misir Ali.

title: Himu Rachanasamagra
author: Humayun Ahmed
description: Complete stories of Himu, the barefoot philosopher who wanders Dhaka in a yellow panjabi.

title: Shongkhonil Karagar
author: Humayun Ahmed
description: A haunting liberation war novel depicting the horrors of 1971 with deeply human characters.

title: Nondito Naroke
author: Humayun Ahmed
description: Humayun Ahmed's debut novel set against family drama and the liberation war backdrop.

title: Chilekotha Sepoai
author: Akhteruzzaman Elias
description: A seminal Bangladeshi novel weaving the 1969 uprising with the lives of ordinary Dhaka dwellers.

title: Matir Jahaj
author: Shahidul Zahir
description: A lyrical novel about memory, city life and the haunting weight of history in Bangladesh.

title: Alal O Dulal
author: Pyarichand Mitra
description: One of the earliest Bengali novels, a satirical social comedy of 19th-century Calcutta life.

title: Devdas
author: Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay
description: The timeless tragic love story of Devdas and Paro, consumed by passion and self-destruction.

title: Parineeta
author: Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay
description: A tender novel of love, sacrifice and social class in early twentieth-century Bengal.

title: Rajlakshmi O Srikanta
author: Sarat Chandra Chattopadhyay
description: The beloved story of Srikanta's enduring bond with the enigmatic and free-spirited Rajlakshmi.

title: Bigganer Aloy
author: Abdulla Al-Muti
description: An accessible introduction to the wonders of science written for curious Bengali readers of all ages.

title: Rupantar
author: Muhammad Zafar Iqbal
description: Science fiction exploring genetic transformation and the ethical frontiers of modern biotechnology.

title: Kosmos
author: Carl Sagan (trans.)
description: Bengali translation of Sagan's landmark exploration of the universe and humanity's place in it.

title: Brihot Biggan
author: Zafar Iqbal
description: A comprehensive popular-science book covering physics, chemistry and biology for general readers.

title: Shakti O Padartha
author: Abdulla Al-Muti
description: An engaging Bengali exploration of energy, matter and the fundamental laws of physics.

title: Jibon O Bhumondol
author: Binayak Sen
description: A science book connecting ecology, life sciences and environmental issues for Bengali audiences.

title: Biggan O Bigganmona
author: Jamal Nazrul Islam
description: Essays by the eminent Bangladeshi scientist promoting scientific thinking and rational inquiry.

title: Manober Utpatti
author: Charles Darwin (trans.)
description: Bengali translation of Darwin's groundbreaking work on the descent and evolution of humanity.

title: Rohossomoy Brohmandb
author: Stephen Hawking (trans.)
description: Bengali adaptation of Hawking's accessible exploration of time, space and black holes.

title: Jontromanaber Jugey
author: Anisur Rahman
description: A popular-science look at the age of machines, automation and artificial intelligence in Bengali.

title: Jibaner Darshan
author: Aroj Ali Matubbar
description: Rational philosophical essays questioning religious dogma and championing free thought in Bangladesh.

title: Shatta O Kalapurush
author: Aroj Ali Matubbar
description: Matubbar's inquiry into truth, the cosmos and the nature of the divine from a rationalist lens.

title: Manush O Biswas
author: Aroj Ali Matubbar
description: A philosophical exploration of human belief systems, superstition and the quest for reason.

title: Boudh Darshan
author: Dharmananda Kosambi (trans.)
description: An introduction to Buddhist philosophical thought and the teachings of the Dhamma in Bengali.

title: Jiboner Artha
author: Viktor Frankl (trans.)
description: Bengali translation of Frankl's Man's Search for Meaning, exploring purpose through suffering.

title: Probondho Samagra
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: Tagore's collected philosophical essays on life, art, education, religion and human civilization.

title: Darshan Parichiti
author: Gobinda Chandra Dev
description: An accessible Bengali introduction to world philosophy by the martyred Bangladeshi philosopher.

title: Stoicism: Jibon Badol
author: Ryan Holiday (trans.)
description: Bengali adaptation of stoic philosophy for modern readers seeking resilience and practical wisdom.

title: Niti O Manobota
author: Immanuel Kant (trans.)
description: Bengali rendering of Kantian ethics exploring duty, morality and categorical imperatives.

title: Satyagraha
author: Mahatma Gandhi (trans.)
description: Gandhi's philosophy of nonviolent resistance, truth-force and moral courage in Bengali.

title: Bangladesher Itihash
author: Sirajul Islam
description: A comprehensive scholarly history of Bangladesh from ancient times through independence.

title: Ekattorer Dinguli
author: Jahanara Imam
description: The personal diary of a liberation war mother documenting the horrors and heroism of 1971.

title: Muktijuddher Itihas
author: M. R. Akhtar Mukul
description: A detailed historical account of the nine-month liberation war that created Bangladesh.

title: Banglar Itihas
author: Ramesh Chandra Majumdar
description: A scholarly chronicle of Bengal's history spanning from ancient kingdoms to the modern era.

title: Mughal Bharat
author: Jadunath Sarkar (trans.)
description: A Bengali account of the Mughal Empire's rise, grandeur and eventual decline in the subcontinent.

title: Palashi Theke Bangladesh
author: Salimullah Khan
description: An intellectual history tracing the journey from the Battle of Plassey to the birth of Bangladesh.

title: Prithibibyapi Itihas
author: H. G. Wells (trans.)
description: Bengali translation of Wells's sweeping outline of world history from prehistoric times to modernity.

title: Bangabandhu Jibon O Rajniti
author: Moinul Hossain
description: A biographical and political history of Sheikh Mujibur Rahman and the making of Bangladesh.

title: Onnyo Ek Itihash
author: Howard Zinn (trans.)
description: Bengali rendering of Zinn's people's history, centering marginalized voices over rulers and empires.

title: Sultanate O Mughal Shashon
author: Abu Mohammad Habibullah
description: An academic study of Sultanate and Mughal rule in Bengal and its cultural and social legacies.

title: Al Quran: Bangla Anubad
author: Maulana Muhiuddin Khan
description: A widely read and authoritative Bengali translation of the Holy Quran with explanatory notes.

title: Sahih Bukhari
author: Imam Bukhari (trans.)
description: Bengali translation of the most authentic hadith collection, essential for Islamic scholarship.

title: Islam O Adhunik Jiban
author: Abul Ala Maududi
description: Maududi's exploration of how Islamic principles apply to modern personal and social life.

title: Sirat-un-Nabi
author: Shibli Nomani (trans.)
description: A scholarly Bengali biography of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) tracing his life and spiritual mission.

title: Gita O Manab Dharma
author: Swami Vivekananda (trans.)
description: Bengali commentary on the Bhagavad Gita and its relevance to human ethics and self-realization.

title: Dharma O Niti
author: Rabindranath Tagore
description: Tagore's reflections on religion, moral life, and the spiritual dimensions of human existence.

title: Quran Majeed: Tafsir
author: Maulana Ashraf Ali Thanawi (trans.)
description: A widely referenced Bengali tafsir offering detailed explanation and context for Quranic verses.

title: Bishwas O Mukti
author: Jibanananda Das
description: Philosophical poems and prose exploring faith, doubt and the search for spiritual liberation.

title: Hindu Dharmer Parichiti
author: Sarvapalli Radhakrishnan (trans.)
description: Bengali introduction to Hinduism's philosophy, rituals and diverse theological traditions.

title: Bouddho Dharma O Darshan
author: Bhikkhu Buddhaghosha (trans.)
description: A Bengali overview of Buddhist teachings, the Eightfold Path and the path to nirvana.
